name: Crimean Tatars

colors.symbolism: the heavenly blue (kök) of the Turkic peoples; freedom
the tarak tamga in the canton — the dynastic seal of the Geray (Giray) khans of the Crimean Khanate

description: A light-blue field with a yellow tarak tamga (a comb-shaped tribal seal) in the upper hoist.
history: The flag of the Crimean Tatar people was adopted by the first Qurultay of the Crimean Tatar people on 30 June 1991, on the basis of the historic tarak tamga emblem of the Geray (Giray) khans who ruled the Crimean Khanate from 1441 to 1783. The light blue (kök) is a pan-Turkic colour. The flag is the official symbol of the Crimean Tatar national movement, of the Crimean Tatar autonomous self-government under Ukraine, and remains widely flown by Crimean Tatars in the diaspora and in exile from Russian-occupied Crimea since 2014.

features.role: tarak tamga — historic seal of the Geray khans of the Crimean Khanate
features.symbolism: the dynastic seal of the Crimean Khanate (1441–1783)
